session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8513 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by fetching the PR feedback and classifying it according to the model provided in the skill documentation.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "No git diff available"
Check for file restructuring in the PR

session.tool_result:

          "author": "github-actions",
          "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy detected.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py:230`

This code block copies a partial excerpt (`sorted(session_xml_dir.glob("*.xml"))`) from erk source. Per the One Code Rule, partial excerpts are not an exception and will go stale if the source changes.

Suggested fix: Replace with the source pointer format from `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md` (HTML comment + prose reference). Describe the deterministic ordering behavior in prose instead.",
          "created_at": "2026-03-01T20:35:25Z"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],

      "body": "<!-- audit-pr-docs -->

## ❌ Audit PR Docs

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 12:35:29 PT

Found 2 violations across 2 files. 1 inline comment posted. 1 pre-existing violation noted below (cannot attach inline comment to unchanged line).

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
❌ Verbatim source code copy — Found in `docs/learned/planning/learn-session-embedding.md:17`
⚠️ Verbatim source code copy (pre-existing, unchanged line) — Found in `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:66`
✅ Inaccurate claims — None found in current content
✅ Drift risk (line numbers in prose) — None found in new content

### Violations Summary
- `docs/learned/planning/learn-session-embedding.md:17`: Partial excerpt `sorted(session_xml_dir.glob("*.xml"))` copied from `plan_save.py:230`. Replace with prose description + source pointer.
- `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md:66` (pre-existing): Verbatim try/except block from `land_learn.py:188-191`. The surrounding prose already describes the fire-and-forget behavior — the code block is redundant and will go stale.

### Files Reviewed
- `docs/learned/architecture/index.md`: 0 violations (index-only change)
- `docs/learned/architecture/inference-hoisting.md`: 0 violations — new section follows correct patterns; CLI invocation example at line 137 is an allowed exception
- `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md`: 1 pre-existing violation (verbatim try/except at line 66)
- `docs/learned/planning/index.md`: 0 violations (index-only change)
- `docs/learned/planning/learn-session-embedding.md`: 1 violation (partial code excerpt at line 17)
- `docs/learned/tui/index.md`: 0 violations (index-only change)
- `docs/learned/tui/one-shot-prompt-modal.md`: 0 violations — design decision sections are high-value; CLI invocation example is an allowed exception
- `docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md`: 0 violations — data flow diagram is conceptual; stale-data rationale is high-value

### Verdict Summary
| File | Verdict | Issues |
|------|---------|--------|
| `docs/learned/architecture/inference-hoisting.md` | KEEP | 0 |
| `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md` | SIMPLIFY | 1 verbatim (pre-existing) |
| `docs/learned/planning/learn-session-embedding.md` | SIMPLIFY | 1 verbatim |
| `docs/learned/tui/one-shot-prompt-modal.md` | KEEP | 0 |
| `docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md` | KEEP | 0 |

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-01 12:35:29 PT**: Found 2 violations (1 new verbatim partial excerpt in learn-session-embedding.md:17; 1 pre-existing verbatim try/except in land-learn-integration.md:66). 1 inline comment posted, 1 noted in summary only.

</details>",
